spec_match.rationale: Encodes a data URL, fetches it, reads `blob.type`, and compares sorted parameter lists — the tightest regression test possible for the spec's parameter-preservation requirement.

interactive.rationale: Five presets covering charset / boundary / codecs / profile / nonstandard parameters, a freeform MIME + body editor, build-and-roundtrip plus copy-data-URL buttons, and a PASS/PARTIAL/FAIL verdict box driven by actual blob.type comparison.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a data URL roundtrip producing a Blob.type and a PASS/PARTIAL/FAIL verdict — exactly what the page does.

edge_cases.rationale: Five presets explicitly cover the spec's harder cases: ISO-8859-1 charset, multipart boundary, quoted codec list, complex profile URN, and multi-parameter custom MIME. Sorted-comparison protects against parameter-order differences. This is the most thorough edge-case coverage in this feature folder.

references.rationale: See-also section links to RFC 2397, WHATWG MIME Sniffing, and https://chromestatus.com/feature/4874471565557760.

design_system.rationale: Variables-driven, verdict-card classes use existing accent-* tokens (pass/fail/warn), no static-card.

openQuestions.title: Add a parameter-value-quoting edge case
openQuestions.detail: The video/mp4 preset uses double-quoted parameter values (`codecs="avc1..."`). Add a preset that includes a quoted value containing a comma or semicolon (the spec edge-case for parameter quoting) so visitors see the parser handles MIME quoting rules correctly.

openQuestions.title: Test parameter-name case insensitivity
openQuestions.detail: Per RFC 7231, parameter names are case-insensitive. Run a roundtrip with `Charset=UTF-8` (capital C) and assert the verdict still reports PASS — that's a real-world wart for authors who copy/paste from server headers.

openQuestions.title: Note clipboard permission failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The copy-data-URL button silently swallows the clipboard error with `try { await navigator.clipboard.writeText(url); } catch {}`. Surface a small success / failure indicator so visitors know whether the copy worked, especially in cross-origin iframes where the API is gated.

summary: The strongest concept in this feature folder — actually executes the spec's preservation guarantee as a regression test with five well-chosen presets and a parameter-sorted verdict. Add parameter-quoting and case-insensitivity edge cases to make it truly exhaustive.
